explanation: The requirement states that 'prop_0' should never be true at any point in any execution of the system. In LTL, this is represented by globally not prop_0, i.e., 'G (!prop_0)'.
The requirement states that 'prop_0' should never occur at any point. In LTL, this is expressed using the G (globally) operator and negation, to specify that at all times, 'prop_0' is false.
The requirement states that the system shall not satisfy prop_0 at any time. In LTL, this is expressed as G !prop_0, meaning globally (always), prop_0 does not hold.

The requirement is that prop_0 should never happen at any point in time. In LTL, this is represented using the 'globally' operator G, which specifies that a property holds at every moment on the path. Therefore, G !prop_0 expresses that prop_0 is always false.
The requirement states that prop_0 should never occur. In LTL, this is expressed by stating that globally (always), prop_0 is not true.
The requirement specifies that 'prop_0' is never true at any point in time. This is best expressed in LTL using the globally (G) operator and negation.
